'''Sebastine Ikahihifo''' (born 27 January 1991) is a New Zealand professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} and {{rlp|lf}}, most recently for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>[https://www.giantsrl.com/article/9016/retained-list-confirmed RETAINED LIST CONFIRMED]</ref>

He previously played for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]].

==Background==
Ikahihifo was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand, and is of [[Tongan people|Tongan]] descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.theroar.com.au/2015/09/12/tonga-name-58-man-squad-for-cook-islands-qualifier/|title=Tonga name 58-man squad for Cook Islands qualifier|publisher=theroar.com.au|date=12 September 2015|accessdate=30 December 2015}}</ref>

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Mangere East Hawks]].{{citation needed|date=May 2022}}

==Playing career==
===Early career===
[[File:SEBASTINE IKAHIHIFO.jpg|thumb|right|Ikahihifo playing for the Warriors in 2009]]
He signed for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in 2010 and played 36 games in the [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|Toyota Cup]], being part of the 2010 and 2011 grand final victories. In 2012, Ikahihifo played for the [[Auckland Vulcans]] in the [[NSW Cup]].

===2012===
In round 21 of the [[2012 NRL season]], Ikahihifo made his first grade début for the New Zealand Warriors against the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] playing off the interchange bench in the Warriors' 24–22 loss at [[NIB Stadium]] in [[Perth, Western Australia|Perth]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/manly-sea-eagles-take-on-warriors-at-patersons-stadium-in-round-21-of-nrl-season/story-fn2mcuj6-1226437602460|title=Manly Sea Eagles stage remarkable comeback to break Warriors' hearts at Patersons Stadium in Perth|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|accessdate=2015-09-26}}</ref> Ikahihifo played in 4 matches for the Warriors in the [[2012 NRL season]].

===2013===
[[File:SEBASTINE IKAHIHIFO - 8918467046.jpg|thumb|right|Ikahihifo playing for the [[Auckland Vulcans]] in 2013]]
Ikahihifo finished the [[2013 NRL season]] with him playing in 8 matches for the Warriors.

===2014===
In February 2014, Ikahihifo was selected in the Warriors' inaugural [[2014 Auckland Nines]] squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|title=Squads For Auckland Nines|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2014-02-14|accessdate=2015-09-26|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160115214258/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|archivedate=2016-01-15}}</ref> In June 2014, Ikahihifo had his contract extended to the end of the 2015 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/warriors-snare-ikahihifo-for-2015/tabid/10874/newsid/79704/default.aspx|title=Warriors snare Ikahihifo for 2015|publisher=NRL.com|date=2014-06-26|accessdate=2015-09-26}}</ref> Ikahihifo finished off the Warriors' [[2014 NRL season]] with him playing in 18 matches.

===2015===
On 16 January, Ikahihifo was named in the Warriors' [[2015 NRL Auckland Nines]] wider training squad but did not make the final squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/warriors-2015-nines-squad/|title=WARRIORS NAME 2015 NINES SQUAD|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2015-01-16|accessdate=2015-09-26|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303212914/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/warriors-2015-nines-squad/|archivedate=2016-03-03}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/warriors-cull-nines-squad/|title=Warriors Cull Back Nines Squad|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2015-01-28|accessdate=2015-09-26}}</ref> In Round 5 against the [[Melbourne Storm]], he scored his first NRL career try in the Warriors' 30–14 loss at [[Melbourne Rectangular Stadium|AAMI Park]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.3news.co.nz/sport/storm-blow-out-warriors-30-14-at-aami-park-2015040706#axzz3l5fnxgvA|title=Storm blow out Warriors 30 14 at AAMI Park | Sport|publisher=3 News|date=2015-04-07|accessdate=2015-09-26}}</ref> He finished off the season having played in 9 matches and scoring a try.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTAtMTY2MC00LS0tLS0tMTk5NzUtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-09-03|accessdate=2015-09-26}}</ref> On 23 September, he signed a two-year contract with the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] starting in 2016.<ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/dragons-sign-warrior-ikahihifo/|title=Dragons Sign Warrior Ikahihifo|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2014-09-14|accessdate=2015-09-26}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=|url=http://www.triplem.com.au/sydney/sport/nrl/news/2015/9/st-george-illawarra-dragons-sign-warriors-sebastine-ikahihifo/|title=St George Illawarra Dragons Sign Warriors Sebastine Ikahihifo | Triple M NRL|publisher=Triplem.com.au|date=|accessdate=2015-09-26}}</ref>

===2016===
On 29 January, Ikahihifo was named in St. George Illawarra's 2016 Auckland Nines squad.<ref>{{cite web|author=Published by Rocco Luca|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/dragons-name-2016-nines-squad/|title=Dragons name 2016 Nines squad|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2016-01-29|accessdate=2016-05-17|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160204025959/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/dragons-name-2016-nines-squad/|archivedate=2016-02-04}}</ref> He was released from his St. George contract without making an appearance for the club in order to join the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]] as a mid-season transfer.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/36296693|title=Sebastine Ikahihifo: Huddersfield Giants sign St George Illawarra Dragons forward - BBC Sport|publisher=Bbc.co.uk|date=1970-01-01|accessdate=2016-05-17}}</ref>

===2017===
In 2017, Ikahihifo was named in the [[Super League Dream Team]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41387829 |title=Castleford Tigers players in team of 2017 season |work=BBC Sport |date=25 September 2017 |access-date=2 April 2026}}</ref>

===2018===
He made a total of 20 appearances for Huddersfield in all competitions as the club missed the finals series finishing 5th.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/53776/season-review-huddersfield-giants/|title=Season Review Huddersfield Giants|website=www.rugby-league.com}}</ref>

===2019===
He made a total of 18 appearances in the 2019 season as Huddersfield finished a disappointing 10th.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/will-they-arise-again-huddersfield-giants-2019-season-review-23227/|title=Will They Arise Again?! Huddersfield Giants 2019 Season Review|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com|date=14 October 2019 }}</ref>

===2020===
He joined [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] on loan in the 2020 season.  He later played for Salford in their [[2020 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat against Leeds.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54555957|title=Leeds beat Salford 17-16 after Luke Gale lands late winning drop-goal|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

===2021===
On 13 November 2020, the club website reported that they would extend the loan period to cover the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=www.salfordreddevils.net|date=13 Nov 2020|accessdate=28 Dec 2020|title=Sebastine Ikahihifo to return in 2021|url=https://www.salfordreddevils.net/sebastine-ikahihifo-to-return-in-2021/}}</ref>

===2022===
He played a total of 13 matches for Huddersfield in the 2022 Super League season as Huddersfield reached the playoffs. He played in Huddersfield's shock 28–0 loss to Salford in the elimination playoff.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/huddersfield-vs-salford/57781#:~:text=Huddersfield%20Giants%200%2D28%20Salford,%E2%80%93%20Marc%20Sneyd%20(6)./|title=Super League: Salford Red Devils into semi-finals after beating Huddersfield Giants 28–0|website=www.skysports.com|accessdate=7 October 2022}}</ref>

===2023===
He played 21 matches with Huddersfield in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished ninth on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ian-watson-explains-why-huddersfield-giants-underperformed-in-super-league-2023/#:~:text=HUDDERSFIELD%20GIANTS%20have%20been%20one,silverware%20once%20more%20this%20season./|title=Ian Watson explains why Huddersfield Giants underperformed in Super League 2023|website=www.totalrl.com|date=29 September 2023 }}</ref>

===2024===
Ikahihifo played 24 games for Huddersfield in the 2024 Super League season as the club finished 9th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants-34-castleford-tigers-10-hosts-win-dead-rubber-but-2024-season-one-to-forget-for-west-yorkshire-rivals-4789784/|title=Huddersfield Giants 34 Castleford Tigers 10: Hosts win dead rubber but 2024 season one to forget for West Yorkshire rivals|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>
